root_text.an6.53:1.1: Atha kho aññataro brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an6.53:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.53:2.1: “Atthi nu kho, bho gotama, eko dhammo bhāvito bahulīkato yo ubho atthe samadhiggayha tiṭṭhati—diṭṭhadhammikañceva atthaṁ, yo ca attho samparāyiko”ti?
root_text.an6.53:2.2: “Atthi kho, brāhmaṇa, eko dhammo bhāvito bahulīkato yo ubho atthe samadhiggayha tiṭṭhati—diṭṭhadhammikañceva atthaṁ, yo ca attho samparāyiko”ti.
root_text.an6.53:3.1: “Katamo pana, bho gotama, eko dhammo bhāvito bahulīkato yo ubho atthe samadhiggayha tiṭṭhati—diṭṭhadhammikañceva atthaṁ, yo ca attho samparāyiko”ti?
root_text.an6.53:3.2: “Appamādo kho, brāhmaṇa, eko dhammo bhāvito bahulīkato ubho atthe samadhiggayha tiṭṭhati—diṭṭhadhammikañceva atthaṁ, yo ca attho samparāyiko.
root_text.an6.53:4.1: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, yāni kānici jaṅgalānaṁ pāṇānaṁ padajātāni, sabbāni tāni hatthipade samodhānaṁ gacchanti; hatthipadaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati, yadidaṁ mahantattena.
root_text.an6.53:4.2: Evamevaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, appamādo eko dhammo bhāvito bahulīkato ubho atthe samadhiggayha tiṭṭhati—diṭṭhadhammikañceva atthaṁ, yo ca attho samparāyiko.
root_text.an6.53:5.1: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, kūṭāgārassa yā kāci gopānasiyo sabbā tā kūṭaṅgamā kūṭaninnā kūṭasamosaraṇā, kūṭaṁ tāsaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.an6.53:6.1: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, pabbajalāyako pabbajaṁ lāyitvā agge gahetvā odhunāti nidhunāti nicchādeti;
root_text.an6.53:7.1: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, ambapiṇḍiyā vaṇṭacchinnāya yāni kānici ambāni vaṇṭūpanibandhanāni sabbāni tāni tadanvayāni bhavanti;
root_text.an6.53:8.1: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, ye keci khuddarājāno sabbete rañño cakkavattissa anuyantā bhavanti, rājā tesaṁ cakkavattī aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.an6.53:9.1: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, yā kāci tārakarūpānaṁ pabhā sabbā tā candassa pabhāya kalaṁ nāgghanti soḷasiṁ, candappabhā tāsaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.an6.53:9.2: evamevaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, appamādo eko dhammo bhāvito bahulīkato ubho atthe samadhiggayha tiṭṭhati—diṭṭhadhammikañceva atthaṁ yo ca attho samparāyiko.
root_text.an6.53:10.1: Ayaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, eko dhammo bhāvito bahulīkato ubho atthe samadhiggayha tiṭṭhati—diṭṭhadhammikañceva atthaṁ, yo ca attho samparāyiko”ti.
root_text.an6.53:11.2: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an6.53:1.1: Then a certain brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an6.53:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an6.53:2.1: “Worthy Gotama, is there one thing that, when developed and cultivated, secures benefits for both this life and lives to come?”
translation_text.an6.53:3.2: “Diligence, brahmin, is one thing that, when developed and cultivated, secures benefits for both this life and lives to come.
translation_text.an6.53:4.1: The footprints of all creatures that walk can fit inside an elephant’s footprint. So an elephant’s footprint is said to be the biggest of them all.
translation_text.an6.53:4.2: In the same way, diligence is one thing that, when developed and cultivated, secures benefits for both this life and lives to come.
translation_text.an6.53:5.1: The rafters of a bungalow all lean to the peak, slope to the peak, and meet at the peak, so the peak is said to be the topmost of them all.
translation_text.an6.53:5.2: In the same way, diligence is one thing …
translation_text.an6.53:6.1: A reed-cutter, having cut the reeds, grabs them at the top and shakes them down, shakes them about, and shakes them off.
translation_text.an6.53:6.2: In the same way, diligence is one thing …
translation_text.an6.53:7.1: When the stalk of a bunch of mangoes is cut, all the mangoes attached to the stalk will follow along.
translation_text.an6.53:7.2: In the same way, diligence is one thing …
translation_text.an6.53:8.1: All lesser rulers are vassals of a wheel-turning monarch, so the wheel-turning monarch is said to be the foremost of them all.
translation_text.an6.53:8.2: In the same way, diligence is one thing …
translation_text.an6.53:9.1: The radiance of all the stars is not worth a sixteenth part of the moon’s radiance, so the moon’s radiance is said to be the best of them all.
translation_text.an6.53:9.2: In the same way, diligence is one thing that, when developed and cultivated, secures benefits for both this life and lives to come.
translation_text.an6.53:10.1: This is the one thing that, when developed and cultivated, secures benefits for both this life and lives to come.”
translation_text.an6.53:11.2: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.an6.53:8.1: khuddarājāno → kuḍḍarājāno (bj); khuddakarājāno (sya-all) | anuyantā → anuyuttā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
